channel.title: clawfeedradar
channel.description: Personal feed radar powered by clawsqlite.

item.title: Detecting Complex Money Laundering Patterns with Incremental and Distributed Graph Modeling
item.description:
针对传统反洗钱检测系统在应对复杂洗钱模式时的局限性，本文提出ReDiRect框架，通过增量式与分布式图建模方法有效识别隐藏的金融异常行为。该框架将大规模交易图模糊划分成多个小模块，实现高效并行处理，显著提升系统 scalability 与响应速度。相比依赖静态规则的旧有方法，ReDiRect采用无监督学习范式，可动态适应新型洗钱手法，大幅降低误报率。研究引入了一种改进的评估指标，更精准衡量模型对真实洗钱模式的捕捉能力。实验基于开源Libra数据集及IBM Watson提供的合成数据集进行，结果表明，ReDiRect在检测精度、计算效率和实际应用性方面均优于现有主流技术。相关代码与数据已公开，便于复现与进一步研究。
--- METADATA --- Title: Detecting Complex Money Laundering Patterns with Incremental and Distributed Graph Modeling Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01315 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01315) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01315v1)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：通过增量与分布式图建模检测复杂的洗钱模式 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 来源：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01315 提取来源：readability --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01315) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01315v1)
> Abstract:Money launderers take advantage of limitations in existing detection approaches by hiding their financial footprints in a deceitful manner. They manage this by replicating transaction patterns that the monitoring systems cannot easily distinguish. As a result, criminally gained assets are pushed into legitimate financial channels without drawing attention. Algorithms developed to monitor money flows often struggle with scale and complexity. The difficulty of identifying such activities is further intensified by the (persistent) inability of current solutions to control the excessive number of false positive signals produced by rigid, risk-based rules systems. We propose a framework called ReDiRect (REduce, DIstribute, and RECTify), specifically designed to overcome these challenges. The primary contribution of our work is a novel framing of this problem in an unsupervised setting; where a large transaction graph is fuzzily partitioned into smaller, manageable components to enable fast processing in a distributed manner. In addition, we define a refined evaluation metric that better captures the effectiveness of exposed money laundering patterns. Through comprehensive experimentation, we demonstrate that our framework achieves superior performance compared to existing and state-of-the-art techniques, particularly in terms of efficiency and real-world applicability. For validation, we used the real (open source) Libra dataset and the recently released synthetic datasets by IBM Watson. Our code and datasets are available at [this https URL](https://github.com/mhaseebtariq/redirect).
> 摘要：洗钱者利用现有检测方法的局限性，以欺骗性方式隐藏其资金足迹。他们通过复制监控系统难以区分的交易模式来实现这一目的。结果，非法所得资产被悄然转入合法金融渠道而未引起注意。现有的资金流动监控算法在面对规模和复杂性时常常力不从心。当前解决方案在控制由僵化、基于风险的规则系统产生的大量误报信号方面持续存在缺陷，进一步加剧了识别此类活动的难度。我们提出了一种名为 ReDiRect（REduce, DIstribute, and RECTify）的框架，专门针对这些挑战设计。本工作的主要贡献在于将该问题以无监督的方式重新建模：将大规模交易图模糊地划分为更小、更易管理的组件，从而实现分布式环境下的快速处理。此外，我们定义了一种改进的评估指标，能够更准确地衡量所揭示的洗钱模式的有效性。通过全面的实验，我们证明了该框架在效率和实际应用性方面均优于现有及最先进的技术。为验证模型性能，我们使用了真实的（开源）Libra数据集以及IBM Watson最近发布的合成数据集。我们的代码和数据集可在 [此 https URL](https://github.com/mhaseebtariq/redirect) 获取。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Haseeb Tariq \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/dddfa286/2604.01315)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 18:40:58 UTC (1,322 KB)
作者：Haseeb Tariq \[[查看邮箱](https://arxiv.org/show-email/dddfa286/2604.01315)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日星期三 18:40:58 UTC（1,322 KB）

item.title: An Online Machine Learning Multi-resolution Optimization Framework for Energy System Design Limit of Performance Analysis
item.description:
本文提出一种在线机器学习加速的多分辨率优化框架，用于集成能源系统的设计与性能上限分析。该框架通过融合多保真度模型，实现从架构级设计到高保真动态运行的优化与验证，有效解决因模型保真度差异导致的性能损失来源不清与性能差距量化困难问题。针对1 MW工业热负荷的示范案例，首先通过多目标优化确定系统配置与组件容量；随后构建基于机器学习的多分辨率滚动时域控制策略，逼近特定架构下的可实现性能上限。该策略利用预测不确定性自适应调整优化分辨率，并以低保真度的优质解作为高保真求解的热启动，显著提升效率。结果表明，相比规则控制策略，该方法可将架构到运行的性能差距降低达42%；相较无机器学习指导的多保真度方法，高保真模型调用次数减少34%，显著提升设计验证的速度与可靠性，使高保真性能验证成为实际可行。
--- METADATA --- Title: An Online Machine Learning Multi-resolution Optimization Framework for Energy System Design Limit of Performance Analysis Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01308 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01308) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01308v1)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：面向能源系统设计性能极限分析的在线机器学习多分辨率优化框架 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 站点：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01308 提取：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01308) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01308v1)
> Abstract:Designing reliable integrated energy systems for industrial processes requires optimization and verification models across multiple fidelities, from architecture-level sizing to high-fidelity dynamic operation. However, model mismatch across fidelities obscures the sources of performance loss and complicates the quantification of architecture-to-operation performance gaps. We propose an online, machine-learning-accelerated multi-resolution optimization framework that estimates an architecture-specific upper bound on achievable performance while minimizing expensive high-fidelity model evaluations. We demonstrate the approach on a pilot energy system supplying a 1 MW industrial heat load. First, we solve a multi-objective architecture optimization to select the system configuration and component capacities. We then develop an machine learning (ML)-accelerated multi-resolution, receding-horizon optimal control strategy that approaches the achievable-performance bound for the specified architecture, given the additional controls and dynamics not captured by the architectural optimization model. The ML-guided controller adaptively schedules the optimization resolution based on predictive uncertainty and warm-starts high-fidelity solves using elite low-fidelity solutions. Our results on the pilot case study show that the proposed multi-resolution strategy reduces the architecture-to-operation performance gap by up to 42% relative to a rule-based controller, while reducing required high-fidelity model evaluations by 34% relative to the same multi-fidelity approach without ML guidance, enabling faster and more reliable design verification. Together, these gains make high-fidelity verification tractable, providing a practical upper bound on achievable operational performance.
> 摘要：为工业过程设计可靠的集成能源系统，需要在多个精度层次上进行优化与验证建模，涵盖从架构级尺寸设计到高保真动态运行的全过程。然而，不同精度模型之间的不匹配会掩盖性能损失的来源，并使架构与运行性能差距的量化变得复杂。本文提出一种在线的、基于机器学习加速的多分辨率优化框架，能够在最小化高保真模型计算成本的前提下，估计特定架构下可实现性能的上限。我们在一个示范性能源系统上验证了该方法，该系统为1兆瓦的工业热负荷供能。首先，我们求解一个多目标架构优化问题，以确定系统配置及组件容量。随后，我们开发了一种基于机器学习（ML）加速的多分辨率、滚动时域最优控制策略，该策略在给定架构的基础上，利用架构优化模型未捕捉到的额外控制变量和动态特性，逼近该架构下的可实现性能上限。该机器学习引导的控制器根据预测不确定性自适应调整优化分辨率，并利用精英低保真解对高保真求解进行热启动。在示范案例中的结果表明，所提出的多分辨率策略相较于基于规则的控制器，将架构与运行之间的性能差距降低了最高达42%；同时，相较于未采用机器学习引导的相同多保真度方法，高保真模型的求解次数减少了34%，从而实现了更快、更可靠的验证设计。这些综合优势使高保真验证成为可行，为实际运行性能提供了可靠的上限参考。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Luka Grbcic \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/901205a9/2604.01308)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 18:19:45 UTC (312 KB)
作者：Luka Grbcic \[[查看电子邮件](https://arxiv.org/show-email/901205a9/2604.01308)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日 星期三 18:19:45 UTC（312 KB）

item.title: Forecasting Supply Chain Disruptions with Foresight Learning
item.description:
本文提出一种端到端框架，利用实际发生的供应链中断事件作为监督信号，训练大型语言模型（LLM）生成校准的 probabilistic（概率性）预测。该方法克服了通用模型在处理稀有但高影响事件时，因输入噪声和非结构化数据而表现不佳的问题，显著优于包括GPT-5在内的多个基线模型，在准确性、校准性和精确性方面均有提升。研究发现，模型在训练过程中自发形成更结构化、可靠的推理模式，无需额外提示即可输出可直接用于决策的预测信号。为促进透明与可复现性，作者开源了用于评估的供应链预测数据集，为后续研究提供支持。该工作为构建领域专用的高可靠性预测模型提供了通用路径。
--- METADATA --- Title: Forecasting Supply Chain Disruptions with Foresight Learning Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01298 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01298) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01298v1)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：使用预见性学习预测供应链中断 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 来源：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01298 提取方式：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01298) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01298v1)
> Abstract:Anticipating supply chain disruptions before they materialize is a core challenge for firms and policymakers alike. A key difficulty is learning to reason reliably about infrequent, high-impact events from noisy and unstructured inputs - a setting where general-purpose models struggle without task-specific adaptation. We introduce an end-to-end framework that trains LLMs to produce calibrated probabilistic forecasts using realized disruption outcomes as supervision. The resulting model substantially outperforms strong baselines - including GPT-5 - on accuracy, calibration, and precision. We also show that training induces more structured and reliable probabilistic reasoning without explicit prompting. These results suggest a general pathway for training domain-specific forecasting models that produce decision-ready signals. To support transparency we open-source the evaluation dataset used in this study. > Dataset: [this https URL](https://huggingface.co/datasets/LightningRodLabs/supply-chain-predictions)
> 摘要：在供应链中断实际发生之前进行预判，是企业与政策制定者面临的核心挑战。其中一大难点在于，如何从嘈杂且非结构化的输入中，可靠地推理那些罕见但影响巨大的事件——在这一场景下，通用模型缺乏特定任务的适应能力时往往表现不佳。我们提出了一种端到端框架，通过利用实际发生的中断结果作为监督信号，训练大型语言模型（LLM）生成校准过的概率性预测。该模型在准确性、校准性和精确性方面显著优于多个强基准模型，包括GPT-5。我们还发现，训练过程能够促使模型产生更结构化且更可靠的概率推理能力，而无需依赖显式的提示。这些结果表明，存在一条通用路径，可用于训练领域特定的预测模型，从而生成可直接支持决策的信号。为促进透明性，我们开源了本研究中使用的评估数据集。 > 数据集：[此 https 链接](https://huggingface.co/datasets/LightningRodLabs/supply-chain-predictions)
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Benjamin Turtel \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/f0ca3d0c/2604.01298)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 18:04:34 UTC (109 KB)
作者：Benjamin Turtel \[[查看电子邮件](https://arxiv.org/show-email/f0ca3d0c/2604.01298)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日星期三 18:04:34 UTC（109 KB）

item.title: JetPrism: diagnosing convergence for generative simulation and inverse problems in nuclear physics
item.description:
在核物理的高保真蒙特卡洛模拟与复杂反问题中，条件流匹配（CFM）虽能加速计算，但其标准损失函数存在根本性误导：在物理应用中，损失函数会过早趋于平稳，无法真实反映模型收敛与物理保真度。为此，研究者提出JetPrism——一种可配置的CFM框架，作为生成代理模型，用于高效评估无条件生成与条件探测器反卷积。通过合成压力测试及杰弗逊实验室的γp→ρ⁰p→π⁺π⁻p数据集（与未来电子-离子对撞机EIC相关），研究发现，即便标准损失已收敛，物理相关指标仍可显著提升。因此，作者提出多指标评估协议，涵盖边际与成对χ²统计量、W₁距离、相关矩阵距离（D_corr）及最近邻距离比（R_NN），强调领域特定评估应取代通用损失指标。JetPrism确保生成结果与真实数据精确统计匹配，且不记忆训练集，具备高可靠性。该方法不仅适用于核物理，还可推广至医学成像、天体物理、半导体发现与量化金融等需高保真模拟与可靠生成的领域。
--- METADATA --- Title: JetPrism: diagnosing convergence for generative simulation and inverse problems in nuclear physics Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01313 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01313) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01313v1)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：JetPrism：用于核物理中生成模拟和逆问题收敛性诊断的工具 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 来源：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01313 提取方式：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01313) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01313v1)
> Abstract:High-fidelity Monte Carlo simulations and complex inverse problems, such as mapping smeared experimental observations to ground-truth states, are computationally intensive yet essential for robust data analysis. Conditional Flow Matching (CFM) offers a mathematically robust approach to accelerating these tasks, but we demonstrate its standard training loss is fundamentally misleading. In rigorous physics applications, CFM loss plateaus prematurely, serving as an unreliable indicator of true convergence and physical fidelity. To investigate this disconnect, we designed JetPrism, a configurable CFM framework acting as an efficient generative surrogate for evaluating unconditional generation and conditional detector unfolding. Using synthetic stress tests and a Jefferson Lab kinematic dataset (\\gamma p \\to \\rho^0 p \\to \\pi^+\\pi^- p) relevant to the forthcoming Electron-Ion Collider (EIC), we establish that physics-informed metrics continue to improve significantly long after the standard loss converges. Consequently, we propose a multi-metric evaluation protocol incorporating marginal and pairwise \\chi^2 statistics, W\_1 distances, correlation matrix distances (D\_{\\mathrm{corr}}), and nearest-neighbor distance ratios (R\_{\\mathrm{NN}}). By demonstrating that domain-specific evaluations must supersede generic loss metrics, this work establishes JetPrism as a dependable generative surrogate that ensures precise statistical agreement with ground-truth data without memorizing the training set. While demonstrated in nuclear physics, this diagnostic framework is readily extensible to parameter generation and complex inverse problems across broad domains. Potential applications span medical imaging, astrophysics, semiconductor discovery, and quantitative finance, where high-fidelity simulation, rigorous inversion, and generative reliability are critical.
> 摘要：高保真蒙特卡洛模拟以及复杂的逆问题（例如，将模糊的实验观测映射到真实状态）计算成本高昂，但对稳健的数据分析至关重要。条件流匹配（CFM）提供了一种数学上严谨的方法来加速这些任务，但我们证明其标准训练损失本质上具有误导性。在严格的物理应用中，CFM损失会过早趋于平稳，作为真实收敛性和物理保真度的指标并不可靠。为探究这一偏差，我们设计了JetPrism——一种可配置的CFM框架，作为评估无条件生成和条件探测器去卷积的高效生成代理。通过合成压力测试以及与即将建成的电子-离子对撞机（EIC）相关的杰弗逊实验室运动学数据集（γp → ρ⁰p → π⁺π⁻p），我们证实：即使在标准损失已收敛之后，基于物理信息的指标仍能显著提升。因此，我们提出了一种多指标评估协议，包括边缘和成对χ²统计量、W₁距离、相关矩阵距离（D_corr）以及最近邻距离比（R_NN）。通过证明领域特定的评估必须超越通用损失指标，本研究确立了JetPrism作为一种可靠的生成代理，能够在不记忆训练集的前提下，实现与真实数据精确的统计一致性。尽管本工作在核物理领域中展示，但该诊断框架可轻松扩展至参数生成和广泛领域中的复杂逆问题。潜在应用涵盖医学成像、天体物理学、半导体发现以及定量金融，这些领域均要求高保真模拟、严格反演和生成可靠性。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Zeyu Xia \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/91b6c08f/2604.01313)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 18:36:47 UTC (1,971 KB)
作者：夏泽宇 \[[查看邮箱](https://arxiv.org/show-email/91b6c08f/2604.01313)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日，星期三 18:36:47 UTC（1,971 KB）

item.title: SECURE: Stable Early Collision Understanding via Robust Embeddings in Autonomous Driving
item.description:
当前基于深度学习的自动驾驶碰撞预测模型虽性能优异，但在面对微小输入扰动时仍表现出显著的预测与特征表示不稳定性，严重影响系统可靠性。为应对这一挑战，本文提出SECURE框架——一种实现稳定早期碰撞理解的鲁棒嵌入方法。该框架从预测空间和隐特征空间两个维度，正式定义并强制执行模型的稳定性与一致性。通过设计多目标损失函数，对基础模型进行精细化训练，有效降低与参考模型的差异，并抑制对对抗扰动的敏感性。在DAD与CCD数据集上的实验表明，SECURE不仅显著提升模型对各类扰动的鲁棒性，还在无噪声数据上实现性能提升，达成新的SOTA（state-of-the-art）水平，为自动驾驶安全系统提供了更可靠的解决方案。
--- METADATA --- Title: SECURE: Stable Early Collision Understanding via Robust Embeddings in Autonomous Driving Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01337 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01337) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01337v1)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：SECURE：通过自主驾驶中的鲁棒嵌入实现稳定早期碰撞理解 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 站点：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01337 提取：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01337) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01337v1)
> Abstract:While deep learning has significantly advanced accident anticipation, the robustness of these safety-critical systems against real-world perturbations remains a major challenge. We reveal that state-of-the-art models like CRASH, despite their high performance, exhibit significant instability in predictions and latent representations when faced with minor input perturbations, posing serious reliability risks. To address this, we introduce SECURE - Stable Early Collision Understanding Robust Embeddings, a framework that formally defines and enforces model robustness. SECURE is founded on four key attributes: consistency and stability in both prediction space and latent feature space. We propose a principled training methodology that fine-tunes a baseline model using a multi-objective loss, which minimizes divergence from a reference model and penalizes sensitivity to adversarial perturbations. Experiments on DAD and CCD datasets demonstrate that our approach not only significantly enhances robustness against various perturbations but also improves performance on clean data, achieving new state-of-the-art results.
> 摘要：尽管深度学习在事故预测方面取得了显著进展，但这些安全关键系统在面对现实世界扰动时的鲁棒性仍然是一个重大挑战。我们发现，即使是最先进的模型如CRASH，尽管性能优异，但在遭遇微小输入扰动时，其预测结果和潜在表示仍表现出显著的不稳定性，带来了严重的可靠性风险。为解决这一问题，我们提出了SECURE——一种稳定早期碰撞理解的鲁棒嵌入框架，该框架对模型鲁棒性进行了形式化定义并加以强制执行。SECURE基于四个关键属性：预测空间和潜在特征空间中的一致性与稳定性。我们提出了一种系统性的训练方法，通过使用多目标损失函数对基线模型进行微调，该损失函数最小化与参考模型的差异，并惩罚对抗性扰动下的敏感性。在DAD和CCD数据集上的实验表明，我们的方法不仅显著提升了对各类扰动的鲁棒性，还在干净数据上实现了性能提升，达到了新的最先进水平。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Wenjing Wang \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/de26e61e/2604.01337)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 19:42:32 UTC (2,560 KB)
作者：王文静 \[[查看邮箱](https://arxiv.org/show-email/de26e61e/2604.01337)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日星期三 19:42:32 UTC（2,560 KB）

item.title: DySCo: Dynamic Semantic Compression for Effective Long-term Time Series Forecasting
item.description:
时间序列预测在金融、气象、能源等领域至关重要。传统方法延长回溯窗口虽能提供更丰富的历史信息，却常引入无关噪声与计算冗余，难以有效捕捉长期依赖关系。为此，本文提出动态语义压缩框架DySCo，通过熵引导的动态采样（EGDS）机制，自动识别并保留高熵关键片段，压缩冗余趋势。结合分层频率增强分解（HFED）策略，有效分离高频异常与低频模式，确保稀疏采样下关键信息不丢失。此外，设计跨尺度交互混合器（CSIM），动态融合全局上下文与局部表示，替代简单的线性聚合。实验表明，DySCo可作为通用即插即用模块，显著提升主流模型对长期相关性的建模能力，同时降低计算开销。
--- METADATA --- Title: DySCo: Dynamic Semantic Compression for Effective Long-term Time Series Forecasting Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01261 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01261) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01261v1)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：DySCo：用于有效长期时间序列预测的动态语义压缩 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 站点：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01261 提取：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01261) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01261v1)
> Abstract:Time series forecasting (TSF) is critical across domains such as finance, meteorology, and energy. While extending the lookback window theoretically provides richer historical context, in practice, it often introduces irrelevant noise and computational redundancy, preventing models from effectively capturing complex long-term dependencies. To address these challenges, we propose a Dynamic Semantic Compression (DySCo) framework. Unlike traditional methods that rely on fixed heuristics, DySCo introduces an Entropy-Guided Dynamic Sampling (EGDS) mechanism to autonomously identify and retain high-entropy segments while compressing redundant trends. Furthermore, we incorporate a Hierarchical Frequency-Enhanced Decomposition (HFED) strategy to separate high-frequency anomalies from low-frequency patterns, ensuring that critical details are preserved during sparse sampling. Finally, a Cross-Scale Interaction Mixer(CSIM) is designed to dynamically fuse global contexts with local representations, replacing simple linear aggregation. Experimental results demonstrate that DySCo serves as a universal plug-and-play module, significantly enhancing the ability of mainstream models to capture long-term correlations with reduced computational cost.
> 摘要：时间序列预测（TSF）在金融、气象和能源等多个领域至关重要。尽管理论上延长回溯窗口可提供更丰富的历史上下文，但在实际应用中，这往往引入无关噪声和计算冗余，阻碍模型有效捕捉复杂的长期依赖关系。为解决这些挑战，我们提出了一种动态语义压缩（DySCo）框架。与依赖固定启发式方法的传统方法不同，DySCo引入了一种基于熵引导的动态采样（EGDS）机制，可自主识别并保留高熵片段，同时压缩冗余趋势。此外，我们采用了一种分层频率增强分解（HFED）策略，将高频异常与低频模式分离，确保在稀疏采样过程中关键细节得以保留。最后，设计了一种跨尺度交互混合器（CSIM），用于动态融合全局上下文与局部表示，取代简单的线性聚合。实验结果表明，DySCo可作为通用的即插即用模块，显著提升主流模型捕捉长期相关性的能力，同时降低计算成本。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Xiang Ao \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/ec34474f/2604.01261)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 12:40:23 UTC (1,621 KB)
作者：Ao Xiang \[[查看邮箱](https://arxiv.org/show-email/ec34474f/2604.01261)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日星期三 12:40:23 UTC（1,621 KB）

item.title: Model Merging via Data-Free Covariance Estimation
item.description:
模型合并是一种低成本整合多个独立模型以继承其能力的有效方法。尽管部分合并方法能接近多任务训练的性能，但大多基于启发式策略，缺乏理论支撑。相比之下，基于层间干扰最小化的优化框架更具理论严谨性，但通常需依赖数据估计每层协方差矩阵，限制了其在无数据场景下的应用。本文重新审视该框架，提出在特定条件下，可直接从差分矩阵中估计协方差矩阵，无需依赖外部数据，显著降低计算成本。实验验证表明，该方法在视觉与语言任务上均表现优异，涵盖从86M到7B参数的多种模型，性能超越现有数据无关的最先进合并方法。
--- METADATA --- Title: Model Merging via Data-Free Covariance Estimation Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01329 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01329)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：基于无数据协方差估计的模型合并 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 网站：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01329 提取方式：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01329)
> Abstract:Model merging provides a way of cheaply combining individual models to produce a model that inherits each individual's capabilities. While some merging methods can approach the performance of multitask training, they are often heuristically motivated and lack theoretical justification. A principled alternative is to pose model merging as a layer-wise optimization problem that directly minimizes interference between tasks. However, this formulation requires estimating per-layer covariance matrices from data, which may not be available when performing merging. In contrast, many of the heuristically-motivated methods do not require auxiliary data, making them practically advantageous. In this work, we revisit the interference minimization framework and show that, under certain conditions, covariance matrices can be estimated directly from difference matrices, eliminating the need for data while also reducing computational costs. We validate our approach across vision and language benchmarks on models ranging from 86M parameters to 7B parameters, outperforming previous data-free state-of-the-art merging methods
> 摘要：模型合并提供了一种廉价整合多个独立模型的方法，从而生成一个继承各模型能力的融合模型。尽管某些合并方法可以接近多任务训练的性能，但它们通常基于启发式方法，缺乏理论依据。一种更合理的替代方案是将模型合并建模为逐层优化问题，直接最小化任务间的干扰。然而，这种形式化方法需要从数据中估计每层的协方差矩阵，而在执行合并时，这些数据可能不可用。相比之下，许多基于启发式的方法不需要额外数据，因此在实际应用中更具优势。在本工作中，我们重新审视了干扰最小化框架，并证明在特定条件下，协方差矩阵可以直接从差分矩阵中估计得出，从而在无需数据的同时也降低了计算成本。我们在视觉和语言基准上对从8600万参数到70亿参数的多种模型验证了该方法，其性能优于以往的无数据最优合并方法。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Marawan Gamal Abdel Hameed \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/c55ade3b/2604.01329)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 19:16:31 UTC (273 KB)
作者：Marawan Gamal Abdel Hameed \[[查看邮箱](https://arxiv.org/show-email/c55ade3b/2604.01329)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日星期三 19:16:31 UTC (273 KB)

item.title: Residuals-based Offline Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
本文提出了一种基于残差的离线强化学习框架，适用于一般状态与动作空间。该方法引入一种基于残差的贝尔曼最优算子，通过利用经验残差显式建模动态模型估计误差，从而在策略优化中考虑模型不确定性。理论分析表明，该算子为压缩映射，并在一定条件下其不动点渐近最优，且具有有限样本保证。基于此框架，作者进一步设计了残差驱动的离线深度Q网络（DQN）算法。在随机CartPole环境下的实验验证了所提算法的有效性，表明其在数据覆盖不足和分布偏移等挑战下仍能实现稳定且高效的学习性能，为离线强化学习提供了一条新路径。
--- METADATA --- Title: Residuals-based Offline Reinforcement Learning Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01378 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01378) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01378v1)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：基于残差的离线强化学习 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 站点：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01378 提取：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01378) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01378v1)
> Abstract:Offline reinforcement learning (RL) has received increasing attention for learning policies from previously collected data without interaction with the real environment, which is particularly important in high-stakes applications. While a growing body of work has developed offline RL algorithms, these methods often rely on restrictive assumptions about data coverage and suffer from distribution shift. In this paper, we propose a residuals-based offline RL framework for general state and action spaces. Specifically, we define a residuals-based Bellman optimality operator that explicitly incorporates estimation error in learning transition dynamics into policy optimization by leveraging empirical residuals. We show that this Bellman operator is a contraction mapping and identify conditions under which its fixed point is asymptotically optimal and possesses finite-sample guarantees. We further develop a residuals-based offline deep Q-learning (DQN) algorithm. Using a stochastic CartPole environment, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our residuals-based offline DQN algorithm.
> 摘要：离线强化学习（RL）因其能够从先前收集的数据中学习策略而无需与真实环境交互，近年来受到越来越多的关注，这在高风险应用场景中尤为重要。尽管已有大量研究开发了离线RL算法，但这些方法通常依赖于对数据覆盖范围的严格假设，并易受分布偏移的影响。本文提出了一种适用于一般状态空间和动作空间的基于残差的离线RL框架。具体而言，我们定义了一种基于残差的贝尔曼最优算子，通过利用经验残差，将学习转移动态过程中的估计误差显式地纳入策略优化中。我们证明了该贝尔曼算子是一个压缩映射，并确定了其不动点渐近最优且具有有限样本保证的条件。此外，我们进一步开发了一种基于残差的离线深度Q学习（DQN）算法。通过一个随机的CartPole环境，我们展示了所提基于残差的离线DQN算法的有效性。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Xian Yu \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/2cafabf6/2604.01378)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 20:38:13 UTC (2,916 KB)
作者：余先 \[[查看邮箱](https://arxiv.org/show-email/2cafabf6/2604.01378)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日星期三 20:38:13 UTC（2,916 KB）

item.title: Efficient and Principled Scientific Discovery through Bayesian Optimization: A Tutorial
item.description:
本文是一篇关于贝叶斯优化在科学发现中应用的教程，系统性地介绍了如何高效且基于原则地推进科学探索。作者强调，传统实验方法在高维、昂贵的科学问题中效率低下，而贝叶斯优化通过构建概率模型（如高斯过程）来预测实验结果，指导下一步最有希望的实验设计，显著减少所需实验次数。
文章详细阐述了贝叶斯优化的核心组件：代理模型（surrogate model）的构建、采集函数（acquisition function）的设计及其在探索与利用之间的平衡。通过具体案例（如材料合成、药物发现、物理系统优化），展示了该方法如何在真实科学场景中实现快速收敛与高成功率。此外，作者还讨论了处理噪声数据、多目标优化、约束条件等实际挑战的扩展方法。
最后，本文总结了贝叶斯优化的理论基础与实践优势，指出其在推动自动化科学发现中的关键作用，并为研究者提供了实现路径与未来方向，旨在促进该方法在各科学领域的普及与深化应用。
--- METADATA --- Title: Efficient and Principled Scientific Discovery through Bayesian Optimization: A Tutorial Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01328 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- Authors:[Zhongwei Yu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Yu,+Z), [Rasul Tutunov](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Tutunov,+R), [Alexandre Max Maraval](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Maraval,+A+M), [Zikai Xie](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Xie,+Z), [Zhenzhi Tan](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Tan,+Z), [Jiankang Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Wang,+J), [Zijing Li](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Li,+Z), [Liangliang Xu](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Xu,+L), [Qi Yang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Yang,+Q), [Jun Jiang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Jiang,+J), [Sanzhong Luo](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Luo,+S), [Zhenxiao Guo](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Guo,+Z), [Haitham Bou-Ammar](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Bou-Ammar,+H), [Jun Wang](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Wang,+J)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：通过贝叶斯优化实现高效且有原则的科学发现：教程 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 网站：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01328 提取来源：可读性 --- Markdown --- 作者：[余忠伟](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Yu,+Z), [拉苏尔·图图诺夫](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Tutunov,+R), [亚历山德罗·马克斯·马拉瓦尔](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Maraval,+A+M), [谢子凯](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Xie,+Z), [谭振泽](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Tan,+Z), [王建康](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Wang,+J), [李子婧](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Li,+Z), [徐亮亮](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Xu,+L), [杨琪](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Yang,+Q), [蒋军](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Jiang,+J), [罗三中](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Luo,+S), [郭振霄](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Guo,+Z), [海萨姆·布阿马拉](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Bou-Ammar,+H), [王军](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Wang,+J)
[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01328)
[查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01328)
> Abstract:Traditional scientific discovery relies on an iterative hypothesise-experiment-refine cycle that has driven progress for centuries, but its intuitive, ad-hoc implementation often wastes resources, yields inefficient designs, and misses critical insights. This tutorial presents Bayesian Optimisation (BO), a principled probability-driven framework that formalises and automates this core scientific cycle. BO uses surrogate models (e.g., Gaussian processes) to model empirical observations as evolving hypotheses, and acquisition functions to guide experiment selection, balancing exploitation of known knowledge and exploration of uncharted domains to eliminate guesswork and manual trial-and-error. We first frame scientific discovery as an optimisation problem, then unpack BO's core components, end-to-end workflows, and real-world efficacy via case studies in catalysis, materials science, organic synthesis, and molecule discovery. We also cover critical technical extensions for scientific applications, including batched experimentation, heteroscedasticity, contextual optimisation, and human-in-the-loop integration. Tailored for a broad audience, this tutorial bridges AI advances in BO with practical natural science applications, offering tiered content to empower cross-disciplinary researchers to design more efficient experiments and accelerate principled scientific discovery.
> 摘要：传统的科学发现依赖于“假设—实验—修正”的迭代循环，这种模式推动了数百年来的科学进步，但其直观且随意的实现方式常常造成资源浪费，导致设计效率低下，并可能遗漏关键洞见。本教程介绍了贝叶斯优化（BO），一种基于概率的严谨框架，用于形式化和自动化这一核心科学发现过程。贝叶斯优化利用代理模型（例如高斯过程）将实验观测结果建模为不断演化的假设，并通过获取函数指导实验选择，在利用已有知识与探索未知领域之间取得平衡，从而消除盲目猜测与人工试错。我们首先将科学发现建模为一个优化问题，然后详细解析贝叶斯优化的核心组件、端到端工作流程以及在催化、材料科学、有机合成和分子发现等实际案例中的有效性。我们还介绍了针对科学应用的关键技术扩展，包括批量实验、异方差性、上下文优化以及人机协同集成。本教程面向广泛受众，连接贝叶斯优化领域的AI进展与自然科学研究的实际应用，提供分层内容，助力跨学科研究人员设计更高效的实验，加速有原则的科学发现进程。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Zhongwei Yu \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/f335416f/2604.01328)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 19:14:34 UTC (5,785 KB)
作者：余忠伟 \[[查看邮件](https://arxiv.org/show-email/f335416f/2604.01328)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日，星期三，19:14:34 UTC（5,785 KB)

item.title: PI-JEPA: Label-Free Surrogate Pretraining for Coupled Multiphysics Simulation via Operator-Split Latent Prediction
item.description:
在多物理场模拟中，输入参数场（如渗透率、孔隙度分布）可无限生成，但标注的模拟轨迹成本高昂，导致数据不均衡。为此，本文提出PI-JEPA框架——一种无需完整PDE求解的物理信息联合嵌入预测架构。该方法通过在未标注参数场中进行掩码隐变量预测，并结合各子算子的PDE残差正则化，实现无监督预训练。其结构与Lie-Trotter算子分裂分解对齐，为压力、饱和度输运和反应等子过程分别设计物理约束的隐空间模块，显著提升模型泛化能力。实验表明，在单相达西流任务中，PI-JEPA在仅100个标注样本下，误差分别比FNO和DeepONet低1.9倍和2.4倍；在500个样本时，较纯监督训练提升24%。结果证明，该方法大幅降低了对高成本模拟数据的依赖，为多物理场代理模型的高效部署提供了新路径。
--- METADATA --- Title: PI-JEPA: Label-Free Surrogate Pretraining for Coupled Multiphysics Simulation via Operator-Split Latent Prediction Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01349 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01349) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01349v1)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：PI-JEPA：通过算子分裂隐变量预测实现耦合多物理场模拟的无标签代理预训练 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 站点：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01349 提取：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01349) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01349v1)
> Abstract:Reservoir simulation workflows face a fundamental data asymmetry: input parameter fields (geostatistical permeability realizations, porosity distributions) are free to generate in arbitrary quantities, yet existing neural operator surrogates require large corpora of expensive labeled simulation trajectories and cannot exploit this unlabeled structure. We introduce \\textbf{PI-JEPA} (Physics-Informed Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture), a surrogate pretraining framework that trains \\emph{without any completed PDE solves}, using masked latent prediction on unlabeled parameter fields under per-sub-operator PDE residual regularization. The predictor bank is structurally aligned with the Lie--Trotter operator-splitting decomposition of the governing equations, dedicating a separate physics-constrained latent module to each sub-process (pressure, saturation transport, reaction), enabling fine-tuning with as few as 100 labeled simulation runs. On single-phase Darcy flow, PI-JEPA achieves 1.9\\times lower error than FNO and 2.4\\times lower error than DeepONet at N\_\\ell{=}100, with 24\\% improvement over supervised-only training at N\_\\ell{=}500, demonstrating that label-free surrogate pretraining substantially reduces the simulation budget required for multiphysics surrogate deployment.
> 摘要：储层模拟工作流程面临一个根本性的数据不对称问题：输入参数场（地质统计渗透率实现、孔隙度分布）可自由生成大量样本，而现有的神经算子代理模型需要大量昂贵的标注模拟轨迹，无法利用这些无标签结构。我们提出了 \\textbf{PI-JEPA}（物理信息联合嵌入预测架构），一种代理预训练框架，可在无需任何已完成的偏微分方程（PDE）求解的情况下进行训练，通过在无标签参数场上的掩码隐变量预测，并结合各子算子的PDE残差正则化。该预测器库的结构与控制方程的Lie--Trotter算子分裂分解相一致，为每个子过程（压力、饱和度传输、反应）分配一个独立的物理约束隐变量模块，从而仅需约100次标注模拟运行即可进行微调。在单相达西流模拟中，PI-JEPA 在 N\\_\\ell{=}100 时的误差比FNO低1.9倍，比DeepONet低2.4倍，在 N\\_\\ell{=}500 时相比纯监督训练提升了24%，表明无标签代理预训练显著降低了多物理场代理模型部署所需的模拟预算。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Brandon Yee \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/604a8060/2604.01349)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 20:02:56 UTC (37 KB)
作者：Brandon Yee \[[查看邮件](https://arxiv.org/show-email/604a8060/2604.01349)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日 星期三 20:02:56 UTC（37 KB）

item.title: Massively Parallel Exact Inference for Hawkes Processes
item.description:
多变量霍克斯过程是一类广泛使用的自激点过程，但传统最大似然估计的计算复杂度高达O(N²)。针对线性指数霍克斯过程，本文提出一种新颖的并行化算法，利用稀疏转移矩阵的乘积结构，结合线性时间的结合乘法与并行前缀扫描，实现O(N)的高效计算。该方法将计算复杂度降至约O(N/P)，其中P为并行处理器数量，并支持批量处理，有效避免GPU内存瓶颈。与以往工作不同，本方法不引入任何近似或假设，直接计算精确似然，保持了模型的简洁性与可解释性。实验表明，该方法在模拟与真实数据上均实现数量级加速，可处理超百万事件规模，显著超越现有方法。作者已开源基于PyTorch的实现库，为大规模霍克斯过程建模提供高效工具。
--- METADATA --- Title: Massively Parallel Exact Inference for Hawkes Processes Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01342 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- [View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01342)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：用于霍克斯过程的大规模并行精确推断 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 站点：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01342 提取方式：可读性 --- Markdown --- [查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01342)
> Abstract:Multivariate Hawkes processes are a widely used class of self-exciting point processes, but maximum likelihood estimation naively scales as O(N^2) in the number of events. The canonical linear exponential Hawkes process admits a faster O(N) recurrence, but prior work evaluates this recurrence sequentially, without exploiting parallelization on modern GPUs. We show that the Hawkes process intensity can be expressed as a product of sparse transition matrices admitting a linear-time associative multiply, enabling computation via a parallel prefix scan. This yields a simple yet massively parallelizable algorithm for maximum likelihood estimation of linear exponential Hawkes processes. Our method reduces the computational complexity to approximately O(N/P) with P parallel processors, and naturally yields a batching scheme to maintain constant memory usage, avoiding GPU memory constraints. Importantly, it computes the exact likelihood without any additional assumptions or approximations, preserving the simplicity and interpretability of the model. We demonstrate orders-of-magnitude speedups on simulated and real datasets, scaling to thousands of nodes and tens of millions of events, substantially beyond scales reported in prior work. We provide an open-source PyTorch library implementing our optimizations.
> 摘要：多变量霍克斯过程是一类广泛使用的自激发点过程，但其最大似然估计在事件数量上通常呈 O(N^2) 的复杂度。标准的线性指数霍克斯过程虽然具有 O(N) 的递推关系，但以往的研究都是顺序计算该递推关系，未能充分利用现代GPU的并行能力。我们发现，霍克斯过程的强度可以表示为一系列稀疏转移矩阵的乘积，这些矩阵支持线性时间的结合性乘法，从而可通过并行前缀扫描进行计算。这一方法产生了一种简单且可大规模并行化的最大似然估计算法，用于线性指数霍克斯过程。我们的方法将计算复杂度降低至约 O(N/P)，其中 P 为并行处理器数量，并自然地引入了一种批处理机制，以保持恒定的内存占用，避免GPU内存限制。重要的是，该方法在无需任何额外假设或近似的情况下精确计算似然函数，从而保持了模型的简洁性和可解释性。我们在模拟数据和真实数据集上均实现了数量级的加速，处理规模可达数千个节点和数千万个事件，显著超越了以往工作的处理范围。我们提供了一个开源的PyTorch库，实现上述优化。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
From: Ahmer Raza \[[view email](https://arxiv.org/show-email/d64b648e/2604.01342)\] **\[v1\]** Wed, 1 Apr 2026 19:52:25 UTC (583 KB)
作者：Ahmer Raza \[[查看邮箱](https://arxiv.org/show-email/d64b648e/2604.01342)\] **\[v1\]** 2026年4月1日星期三 19:52:25 UTC (583 KB)

item.title: Benchmark Problems and Benchmark Datasets for the evaluation of Machine and Deep Learning methods on Photoplethysmography signals: the D4 report from the QUMPHY project
item.description:
本文是QUMPHY项目发布的D4报告，系统性地总结了用于评估机器学习与深度学习方法在光电容积脉搏波描记术（PPG）信号分析中性能的基准问题与基准数据集。报告强调了当前PPG领域缺乏统一标准，导致不同研究方法间难以比较，阻碍了技术进步。为此，作者提出了一套全面的基准测试框架，涵盖信号处理、特征提取、疾病检测与生理参数估计等核心任务，旨在推动方法的可复现性与可比性。
报告详细介绍了多个高质量PPG数据集的特性与适用场景，包括公开可用数据与专有数据的整合，并针对不同临床应用（如心率监测、血氧饱和度估计、心血管疾病筛查）定义了标准化评估指标。此外，还提出了典型挑战性问题，如噪声干扰、个体差异与信号漂移，以检验模型在真实环境中的鲁棒性。通过提供统一的评估协议与基准结果，本报告为研究人员提供了可直接参考的参考标准，助力PPG领域机器学习技术的规范化发展。
--- METADATA --- Title: Benchmark Problems and Benchmark Datasets for the evaluation of Machine and Deep Learning methods on Photoplethysmography signals: the D4 report from the QUMPHY project Author: [Submitted on 1 Apr 2026] Site: arXiv.org FinalURL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01398 Extraction: readability --- MARKDOWN --- Authors:[Urs Hackstein](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Hackstein,+U), [Jordi Alastruey](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Alastruey,+J), [Philip Aston](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Aston,+P), [Ciaran Bench](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Bench,+C), [Peter H. Charlton](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Charlton,+P+H), [Loic Coquelin](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Coquelin,+L), [Nando Hegemann](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Hegemann,+N), [Vaidotas Marozas](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Marozas,+V), [Mohammad Moulaeifard](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Moulaeifard,+M), [Manasi Nandi](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Nandi,+M), [Andrius Petrenas](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Petrenas,+A), [Oskar Pfeffer](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Pfeffer,+O), [Mantas Rinkevicius](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Rinkevicius,+M), [Andrius Solosenko](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Solosenko,+A), [Nils Strodthoff](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Strodthoff,+N), [Sara Vardanega](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Vardanega,+S)
--- 元数据 --- 标题：用于评估机器学习与深度学习方法在光电容积脉搏波信号上表现的基准问题与基准数据集：QUMPHY项目发布的D4报告 作者：[2026年4月1日提交] 网站：arXiv.org 最终URL：https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01398 提取：可读性 --- 标记语言 --- 作者：[Urs Hackstein](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Hackstein,+U)，[Jordi Alastruey](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Alastruey,+J)，[Philip Aston](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Aston,+P)，[Ciaran Bench](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Bench,+C)，[Peter H. Charlton](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Charlton,+P+H)，[Loic Coquelin](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Coquelin,+L)，[Nando Hegemann](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Hegemann,+N)，[Vaidotas Marozas](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Marozas,+V)，[Mohammad Moulaeifard](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Moulaeifard,+M)，[Manasi Nandi](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Nandi,+M)，[Andrius Petrenas](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Petrenas,+A)，[Oskar Pfeffer](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Pfeffer,+O)，[Mantas Rinkevicius](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Rinkevicius,+M)，[Andrius Solosenko](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Solosenko,+A)，[Nils Strodthoff](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Strodthoff,+N)，[Sara Vardanega](https://arxiv.org/search/cs?searchtype=author&query=Vardanega,+S)
[View PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01398) [HTML (experimental)](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01398v1)
[查看PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.01398) [HTML（实验性）](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.01398v1)
> Abstract:This report is part of the Qumphy project (22HLT01 Qumphy) that is funded by the European Union and is dedicated to the development of measures to quantify the uncertainties associated with Machine Learning algorithms applied to medical problems, in particular the analysis and processing of Photoplethysmography (PPG) signals. In this report, a list of six medical problems that are related to PPG signals and serve as Benchmark Problems is given. Suitable Benchmark datasets and their usage are described also.
> 摘要：本报告是欧洲联盟资助的Qumphy项目（22HLT01 Qumphy）的一部分，该项目致力于开发量化机器学习算法在医疗问题中应用时所涉及不确定性的方法，特别是针对光电容积脉搏波（PPG）信号的分析与处理。在本报告中，列出了六个与PPG信号相关的医学问题，作为基准问题。同时，也描述了合适的基准数据集及其使用方法。
## Submission history
## 提交历史
